_provenance: retinoids/treatment_actives/active_conflicts copied from src/recommendation/eligibility.py (D-005/D-029 lineage); do not edit from memory
_retinoid_authority: This file is the ONLY place a retinoid is named. inci.RETINOID_MARKERS is derived from retinoids + retinoid_inci_stems at import, so the pregnancy gate's two arms (actives & knowledge.retinoids, contains_retinoid over raw INCI) cannot disagree. A new retinoid goes in `retinoids` if it is a canonical active id, and in `retinoid_inci_stems` only if it is a label spelling that never parses to one. Drug rows carry inci: [], so for them `retinoids` is the whole gate. Every entry in `retinoids` must also appear in treatment_actives, session_preferences.pm_pinned_actives and gentle.excluded_actives; recsys/tests/test_inci.py enforces this.
_prescription_actives_authority: Drug-catalog actives a profile may truthfully declare in current_actives that are neither canonical INCI actives nor retinoids. contracts.DECLARABLE_ACTIVE_IDS derives from CANONICAL_ACTIVES + retinoids + this list, so the profile vocabulary cannot lag the actives import_drug_catalog mints into product.actives (the duplicate-active gate intersects the two; a declarable gap silences it for exactly the users on the most dangerous actives). sulfur is OTC, not Rx, but rides here for the same reason: it is in the drug catalog and never parses out of a cosmetic INCI. recsys/tests/test_contracts.py checks this list covers the committed DailyMed pool.

session_preferences._comment: pm_pinned may never appear in AM; am_pinned never in PM; preferences flip when a conflict forces it
